overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a highly targeted, professional social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated online behavior. With a total following of 3,580, his posts consistently attract strong engagement from academic, research, and data science communities. Bluesky demonstrates the highest relative engagement rate per post, while LinkedIn serves as his primary reach channel for major professional announcements like research grants and recruitment.

contentThemes.description: Discussions on detecting coordinated link sharing, disinformation campaigns, and computational methodologies.
Hiring posts for postdocs and recruitment of PhD students in computational social science.
Announcements regarding newly published papers, major research grants, and updates to scientific software like CooRnet.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's high relative engagement rate to drive active peer discussions, request reviews, and run informal Q&As on newly published papers.
Utilize LinkedIn primarily for career opportunities, funding announcements, and institutional updates to maximize absolute reach and corporate/academic networking.
Include direct links to open-source code repositories or publications across all platforms to translate high social engagement into web traffic and citations.

platformInsights.bluesky: Yields the highest relative engagement rate (average of 6.14%), making it exceptionally effective for rapid academic dissemination, peer networking, and student outreach.
platformInsights.mastodon: Attracts a specialized, technical audience that is highly responsive to software tool updates (such as CooRnet v2.0) and methodological discussions.
platformInsights.linkedin: Hosts the largest follower base (1,850) and generates the highest absolute engagement numbers (likes and comments), proving essential for career, funding, and institutional visibility.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
